paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph
summary: Conjecture 2.4 from arXiv:1804.01060 states that for every graph H and every forest P of H, every coherent ideal contains a P-filleting of H. The paper's main Theorem 2.3 establishes the case where P is a path of H; the conjecture extends this to all forests. No follow-up paper confirming or disproving the full conjecture was found in a targeted web search covering literature through 2026. The sole internal reference (arXiv:2508.14332) concerns a counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture and is unrelated to the P-filleting conjecture.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirms this paper disproves the weak coarse Menger conjecture (about vertex separators in a coarse analogue of Menger's theorem); it does not address the P-filleting / coherent-ideal conjecture from 1804.01060. The fuzz match (score 69) appears to be a false positive.

search_queries: coherent ideal filleting graph forest conjecture Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl 1804.01060
pure pairs subdivisions graph conjecture filleting forest 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
P-filleting coherent ideal graph theory conjecture "pure pairs" follow-up resolved 2023 2024 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up resolving or disproving the conjecture was found after five web calls. The conjecture is a strengthening of Theorem 2.3 (proven for P a path); the authors note it is best possible since the conclusion fails when P contains a cycle of H. It remains open as of the review date.
